parsed_review.summary: CP00-794 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening — Service Permission And Audit Binding, Risk B, 40 units, RP26.P02.M04.S21-RP26.P02.M05.S30) passes read-only review. Scope is internally consistent: manifest, plan live_state, next-pack-queue, contract latest_pack, and registry.js ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP794_PACK_BINDING all agree on id/range/risk/unit_count and next boundary CP00-795 / RP26.P02.M06.S01. Deliverable distribution (test:6, fixture:2, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:3, implementation:19, contract:1, security_audit:2, ui:3, failure_recovery:2 = 40) and micro_title split (Secondary Workflow Slice:10, Permission And Audit Binding:30) match the manifest units, the SERVICE_IMPLEMENTATION_ROWS row titles (M04 = rows 20-29, M05 = all 30), and the prompt baseline exactly. The CP794 descriptor in service.js follows the established CP788-793 pattern and encodes the correct authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true) and a CP00-795/RP26.P02.M06.S01 handoff. ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is comprehensive and spread into every descriptor/case-set; a grep of packages/enterprise/src found no writeFile/fs/child_process/fetch/http/process.env usage, confirming the descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, runtime-closed claim. validators.js enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, exclusion of tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads, no permission_decision_detail or audit_event_body leakage, no cross-tenant routing, no enterprise-trust claim, plus Hermes/Claude gate and handoff drift checks. The contract (enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.json) is the service.js projection with validation.valid:true / errors:[], latest_projection EnterpriseSaasCp794ServicePermissionAuditBindingDescriptor (CP00-794), and all mandatory_artifacts/no_leak_guards/capabilities/safety_gates/research_alignment present. model.test.js adds three CP794 tests, including explicit M05 assertions that no row evaluates runtime permission, exposes permission decision detail, writes audit events, or leaks audit bodies. command-evidence.json records status=passed with all ten required validation commands plus rp26 validators and git diff --check at exit_code 0 (production_ready:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false pre-review). Review-harness hardening (CP178+) is satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte valid JSON), prompt is 2522 bytes with no embedded diff/source, summary-only review correctly disallowed, no forbidden tools, and no prior/poisoned review receipt exists (status pending). The untracked LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json is correctly classified as unrelated dirty state, not part of this pack. The pack claims only the descriptor-verified layer (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, enterprise trust deferred to human authority); that claim is fully supported. One non-blocking P3 defense-in-depth note on validator row-scan symmetry. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack closeout is not blocked, and goal closeout for this CP is not blocked. Note: commands were not re-executed (read-only, no Bash) — command results are as recorded in command-evidence.json.

findings.title: Descriptor validator row-scan omits active-write permission/audit flags (compensated by unit test)
findings.details: validateDescriptor's row scan (validators.js:137-150) rejects rows that leak permission_decision_detail_included or audit_event_body_included, but does not independently reject rows that assert active runtime writes such as evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, writes_permission_decision, or appends_audit_trace === true. For this 'Permission And Audit Binding' pack those active-verb closures are currently guaranteed only by (a) hard-coded false values in ROW_EXTRAS (permission_precheck/audit_hint_precheck), (b) the descriptor/case-set-level NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread, and (c) an explicit assertion in model.test.js:282-283 scoped to RP26.P02.M05 rows. A hypothetical future row setting evaluates_runtime_permission:true would still pass the contract validator (npm run rp26:enterprise-saas:validate) and would be caught only by the unit test. This does not affect CP00-794: all rows are descriptor-only and the no-write boundary is verified by tests and by command-evidence exit_code 0.
findings.recommendation: Optionally extend the validateDescriptor row scan to also reject evaluates_runtime_permission / writes_audit_event / writes_permission_decision / appends_audit_trace === true (mirroring NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION) so the contract validator enforces the permission/audit no-write boundary independently of the unit test. Non-blocking; safe to defer.
findings.post_review_disposition: Open — informational defense-in-depth; does not block CP00-794 closeout.
